name: Coursera for Business Enterprise Learning API
what_it_does: Coursera for Business enterprise learning platform REST API for access to university-authored and industry-certified courses, skills development programs, and learning analytics at enterprise scale. Enables AI agents to manage enterprise content catalog access and program enrollment for employee learning automation, handle learner seat allocation and provisioning for enterprise license management automation, access skills mapping and learning pathway recommendation for workforce development automation, retrieve course completion and certificate tracking for employee development reporting, manage cohort-based learning programs and group enrollment for organizational learning campaigns, handle learning analytics and completion rate reporting for HR learning effectiveness automation, access industry certification completion (Google, IBM, Meta, AWS) for credential tracking, retrieve credit and degree pathway progress for employer-sponsored education automation, manage content curation from 7,000+ courses for curated learning collection automation, and integrate Coursera with Workday, SuccessFactors, and LMS platforms via SCORM/xAPI and SSO for enterprise HR ecosystem.
use_cases: Upskilling employees from workforce development agents
Tracking certifications from credential management agents
Managing learning licenses from HR provisioning agents
Curating learning paths from skills gap agents
not_for: Custom proprietary course hosting (use internal LMS)
best_when: An enterprise organization wanting AI agents to automate employee access to university and industry-partner courses, skills development program enrollment, learning analytics, and credential tracking within Coursera for Business.
avoid_when: SEAT LICENSE PROVISIONING AND DEPROVISION FOR COST CONTROL: Automated learner provisioning in Coursera for Business consumes enterprise seat licenses; automated provisioning without deprovisioning workflow for terminated or inactive employees creates seat waste and license overrun; implement HRIS departure event-triggered deprovisioning. COMPLETION CERTIFICATE AS PROFESSIONAL CREDENTIAL: Coursera certificates are non-accredited professional development credentials, not state-licensed professional qualifications; automated workforce reporting of Coursera certificate as regulatory compliance training creates credential misrepresentation; distinguish Coursera certificates from accredited CE or regulated training. EMPLOYER-SPONSORED DEGREE TAXABLE BENEFIT TRACKING: Coursera degree pathway access provided by employer as education benefit may be taxable income above IRS §127 exclusion threshold ($5,250/year); automated enrollment in employer-sponsored Coursera degree programs without tax benefit tracking creates payroll tax reporting gap.

auth.notes: Coursera for Business uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Mountain View, California HQ (Coursera, Inc., NYSE: COUR). Founded 2012 by Andrew Ng and Daphne Koller (Stanford). Public company since 2021. 7,000+ courses from 300+ university and industry partners. 148M+ registered learners globally. 14,000+ enterprise customers for Coursera for Business. University content: Yale, Stanford, Duke, Google, IBM, Meta partner courses. Competes with LinkedIn Learning, Udemy Business, and Pluralsight for enterprise skills development.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual seat license subscription for enterprise
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-seat subscription for Coursera for Business; Team (small) and Enterprise (large) tiers; Skills Academy and degree program add-ons
pricing.notes: Mountain View CA. Coursera NYSE:COUR. Founded 2012. 148M+ learners. 14,000+ enterprise customers. Annual per-seat enterprise subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; content catalog and enrollment queries throttled; no webhooks — polling for completion events

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — COMPLETION EVENTS REQUIRE POLLING: Coursera for Business API does not support native webhooks for course completion, certificate issuance, or program completion; automated downstream workflows (HRIS update, badge issuance, recognition) require scheduled polling aligned with expected learning completion timing

SEAT LICENSE DEPROVISIONING FOR COST MANAGEMENT: Automated provisioning must be paired with deprovisioning on employee departure or role change; Coursera for Business charges for all provisioned seats regardless of activity; automated provisioning without HRIS offboarding trigger creates seat cost leakage at enterprise scale

CERTIFICATE DISTINCTION FROM REGULATED CREDENTIALS: Coursera Professional Certificates and industry certificates (Google Project Management, AWS Cloud, IBM Data Science) are non-accredited professional development; automated HR systems must not classify Coursera certificates as regulatory compliance training (OSHA, SEC, state licensing); maintain credential type taxonomy distinguishing Coursera from accredited CE

Content catalog freshness and course retirement — Coursera course catalog changes as university and industry partners add and retire courses; automated learning pathway recommendations based on static course IDs may reference retired courses; implement catalog validity check before automated course assignment

SSO and identity provisioning for large enterprises — Coursera for Business SSO integration (SAML/OIDC) requires IdP configuration per enterprise domain; automated user provisioning via SSO for new hires requires IdP group membership to Coursera license mapping; misconfigured SSO mapping creates access failures for automated onboarding workflows

Degree and academic program enrollment restrictions — Coursera degree programs (online bachelor's, master's) have separate enrollment processes with academic admission requirements; automated enrollment in degree programs is not supported via Business API; degree program access requires direct Coursera academic enrollment separate from enterprise learning automation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise learning platform. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Employee learning and credential data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise learning. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Employee learning and credential data.
